Okay, what's going on guys? So there are two ways to use this supplement order form in the sale. The first way to use this is to actually have it in the clipboards that you're hanging out with their meal plans so that they can write notes and follow along when you're doing the nutrition presentation. At that point, you're not going to actually have them, like when you're explaining to them, you don't want to have them circle that stuff yet. You want them to keep it on the meal plan because it looks like it's much lower commitment, but once they write it on their meal plan, then they will be more likely, after having taken that micro commitment, to then circle it on the supplement order form later, which will kind of be their ticket out of there. You can use that language. Like, cool, just need your supplement order form, that's your ticket out of here and you are good to go. That's kind of how you can conclude some of those meetings. So that is the first way of doing it, works better with large groups so that after you've gone through the actual selling and you've done all the crossoffs and all that stuff, they will already have made the decisions of what they need to get so that when they get to here, it'll be a quick couple of circles and then they fill out their shipping address and then if they don't have the card, they can just bring it up to the front and then you can fill it out for them. So you can just say, hey, if you don't have your card and not a big deal, just bring it up, sign the bottom, I can fill out whatever you have on file later and that's a very easy solution. So that is way number one of using it, which works best with large groups. If you have small groups or individual, that's where using the tablet instead of your form might be easier. But I'm going to just tell you what I have done and we're going to continue to learn this because I didn't have the technology that we have now and so I didn't have the options that you guys now have because of prestige and so this will be kind of a mutual iterative process. But the second way that you can use it is that when you are in the individual sale, you can have them bring up their meal plan and then you can go ahead and just circle those things for them and then just put the total one-time charge at the bottom, circle it and then have them sign. And then you can go out, then you can go and fill in or you have your admin fill in, the remainder of the billing information and the card number. So I hope that makes sense and then someone else can process all their contracts just like normal. So those are the two ways of doing it, just either way you need to make sure that you end up with the someone order form at the end of the day. Now, if you don't want to stack up all of this work, you can just use the tablets but I want to make sure that we have solutions if the Wi-Fi is down or the tablet is in charge or there's too many people and you don't have tablets, whatever it is, I wanted to make sure that this process was covered. And then in terms of how this works, it should be fairly straightforward. They're going to circle the flavor that they need and then they're going to circle the quantity that they need and then the fill in their info and then sign the bottom after the one-time charge has been, make sure that you put that on there. That's really important. I might actually have that one highlighted, circled in red or something. But you're going to want everyone to circle the subscribe and save and the way that you pretty much say that is like, well, if you buy it today, most people just go on subscribe and save because you're going to need it again in 30 days or 28 days, whatever it is, like purposely sound unsure so that it seems like you're not hard selling them. But yeah, so you're going to need this in 28 or 30 days. I'm not really sure what the cycle is, but either way you're going to need it because this is six weeks long. So you can save $27 times two, so $52 by just doing that, which is what most people end up doing. So I would just circle that, you can cancel it later if you want to. It's up to you. Most people don't because stuff's awesome, but either way, it's your call. So I would do that. That's what most people do. And then that's it. So now what are these other three columns here? Or what are all of these columns mean? Consumer is just the inflated retail price that we have that is online and will be substantiated by Amazon pricing so that they can find it and really see that we're selling it at that price. Obviously that is just a price anchor. The great deal it is for them to buy at this price, distributor price at your facility. Now, subscribe and save is, I had to do 11% so that we could get it under $60 because I thought $50 would sound nice. So that's what it is. And then three, six, and 12 months, what those are is this is showing the individual bottle cost for a three month supply. So they can buy three months upfront and pay 57 times three. That's the key here. So they're getting a 12 months supply for $50, they're getting 12 times 50, which is $600, which is what they're paying to get into workout for a year, which honestly is not that bad of a deal. And so you can see that and this is what goes along perfectly with signing someone up for a three, six, 12 month package or like three months for a great with the HDP clients. They just sign up for 12 weeks. Just sell them all three month packages. Done. You know what I mean? Like easy and you add another thousand onto the sale by getting them four things. You don't know what I mean that are a buck 50 each or not four, so it'd be $600, but it's still it's free money. It might as well take it, right? So that is what those are. You can either use it as a tack on behind the meal plan or you can have them with you at the front of the room and then they all they have to do is come up, show you their meal plan and then you just circle for them and have them sign after you total it up.
